<objective>
Scaffold the Vue 3 + Firebase project from scratch and implement all authentication logic, routing guards, and Firestore security rules.

Purpose: Establishes the entire project foundation — build tooling, Firebase connection, auth state management, protected routing, and data security. Everything UI-facing in Plan 02 depends on this infrastructure being correct.

Output: A working (headless) project where auth store actions can be called, router guards enforce authentication, and Firestore rules deny unauthorized access. Tests verify all critical auth behaviors including security rules against the emulator.
</objective>

<tasks>

<task type="auto">
  <name>Task 1: Scaffold Vue 3 project with Firebase, Tailwind, and emulator config</name>
  <files>
    package.json, vite.config.ts, tsconfig.json, tsconfig.app.json, tsconfig.node.json,
    src/main.ts, src/assets/main.css, src/App.vue,
    src/firebase/index.ts,
    firebase.json, firestore.rules, firestore.indexes.json, .firebaserc,
    .env.local.example, .gitignore
  </files>
  <action>
    1. Scaffold the project using `npm create vue@latest . -- --typescript --router --pinia --vitest --eslint --prettier` (the `.` scaffolds in the current directory). If create-vue prompts interactively, use flags to auto-select. After scaffold, verify `package.json` exists.

    2. Install dependencies:
       ```
       npm install firebase
       npm install tailwindcss @tailwindcss/vite
       npm install -D @firebase/rules-unit-testing
       ```

    3. Configure Vite (`vite.config.ts`):
       - Import `vue` from `@vitejs/plugin-vue` and `tailwindcss` from `@tailwindcss/vite`
       - Plugins array: `[vue(), tailwindcss()]`
       - Keep the `@` alias that create-vue generates (maps to `./src`)

    4. Set up Tailwind CSS in `src/assets/main.css`:
       - Replace contents with `@import "tailwindcss";`
       - Remove any other generated CSS files if create-vue created them

    5. Create `src/firebase/index.ts`:
       - Import and call `initializeApp` with config from `import.meta.env.VITE_FIREBASE_*` env vars
       - Export `app`, `auth` (from `getAuth`), and `db` (from `getFirestore`) as singletons
       - In `import.meta.env.DEV` block: dynamically import and call `connectAuthEmulator(auth, 'http://127.0.0.1:9099', { disableWarnings: true })` and `connectFirestoreEmulator(db, '127.0.0.1', 8080)`
       - IMPORTANT: Use top-level await for the dynamic imports in the DEV block. This requires the module to be loaded asynchronously, which Vite handles natively.

    6. Create `.env.local.example` with placeholder values:
       ```
       VITE_FIREBASE_API_KEY=your-api-key
       VITE_FIREBASE_AUTH_DOMAIN=your-project.firebaseapp.com
       VITE_FIREBASE_PROJECT_ID=your-project-id
       VITE_FIREBASE_STORAGE_BUCKET=your-project.firebasestorage.app
       VITE_FIREBASE_MESSAGING_SENDER_ID=your-sender-id
       VITE_FIREBASE_APP_ID=your-app-id
       ```

    7. Create `firebase.json`:
       ```json
       {
         "firestore": {
           "rules": "firestore.rules",
           "indexes": "firestore.indexes.json"
         },
         "emulators": {
           "auth": { "port": 9099 },
           "firestore": { "port": 8080 },
           "ui": { "enabled": true, "port": 4000 }
         }
       }
       ```

    8. Create `firestore.indexes.json`: `{ "indexes": [], "fieldOverrides": [] }`

    9. Create `firestore.rules` with the full deny-by-default + org membership security rules:
       - `rules_version = '2'`
       - Helper functions: `isSignedIn()`, `isOrgMember(orgId)`, `isOrgAdmin(orgId)`
       - `/users/{uid}`: read/write only if `request.auth.uid == uid`
       - `/organizations/{orgId}`: read if `isOrgMember`, write if `isOrgAdmin`
       - `/organizations/{orgId}/members/{uid}`: read if `isOrgMember`, write if `isOrgAdmin`
       - `/organizations/{orgId}/{collection}/{docId}`: read/write if `isOrgMember` (covers songs, services, tasks, events)
       - Catch-all `/{document=**}`: deny all
       - ALSO add a create-only rule: allow authenticated users to create a new org if they are the creator (`request.resource.data.createdBy == request.auth.uid`) and simultaneously create their own admin membership doc. This supports auto-org-creation on first sign-in without a Cloud Function.

    10. Create `.firebaserc`: `{ "projects": { "default": "your-firebase-project-id" } }`

    11. Update `.gitignore` to include: `.env.local`, `.env.*.local`, `node_modules/`, `dist/`, `.firebase/`

    12. Clean up `src/App.vue` to a minimal shell: just `<router-view />` wrapped in a div. Remove all default create-vue template content, demo components, and HelloWorld references. Delete `src/components/HelloWorld.vue`, `src/components/TheWelcome.vue`, `src/components/WelcomeItem.vue`, `src/components/icons/*`, `src/views/HomeView.vue`, `src/views/AboutView.vue` and any other scaffold demo files.

    13. Update `src/main.ts`:
        - Import `./assets/main.css`
        - Import createApp from vue, createPinia from pinia, router from `./router`
        - Create app, use pinia, use router, mount to `#app`
        - Remove any HelloWorld or demo imports
  </action>
  <verify>
    <automated>cd C:/projects/worshipplanner && npx vue-tsc --noEmit 2>&1 | head -20 && echo "---" && npm run build 2>&1 | tail -5</automated>
  </verify>
  <done>
    - `npm run build` succeeds with zero errors
    - `src/firebase/index.ts` exports app, auth, db
    - `firestore.rules` contains isOrgMember helper and deny-by-default catch-all
    - `.env.local.example` documents all required env vars
    - All create-vue demo/scaffold files removed
    - Tailwind CSS imported in main.css
    - `@firebase/rules-unit-testing` installed as dev dependency
  </done>
</task>

<task type="auto" tdd="true">
  <name>Task 2: Implement auth store with all sign-in methods and router guard</name>
  <files>
    src/stores/auth.ts, src/router/index.ts,
    src/stores/__tests__/auth.test.ts, src/router/__tests__/router.test.ts
  </files>
  <behavior>
    Auth store tests (mock firebase/auth):
    - loginWithGoogle: calls signInWithPopup with GoogleAuthProvider; returns user on success; returns null on popup-closed-by-user
    - loginWithEmail: calls signInWithEmailAndPassword; on auth/user-not-found calls createUserWithEmailAndPassword (auto-create)
    - registerWithEmail: calls createUserWithEmailAndPassword directly
    - resetPassword: calls sendPasswordResetEmail with provided email
    - logout: calls signOut; user becomes null
    - isReady: starts false; becomes true after onAuthStateChanged fires
    - isAuthenticated: computed from user !== null
    - ensureUserDocument: after login, creates/updates user doc in Firestore /users/{uid} and auto-creates org + membership if user has no orgIds
    Router guard tests (mock getCurrentUser):
    - Route with meta.requiresAuth redirects to /login when getCurrentUser returns null
    - Route with meta.requiresAuth allows navigation when getCurrentUser returns a user
    - /login route redirects to / when getCurrentUser returns a user (already authenticated)
    - Route without meta.requiresAuth allows navigation regardless of auth state
  </behavior>
  <action>
    **Write tests first (RED):**

    1. Create `src/stores/__tests__/auth.test.ts`:
       - Mock `firebase/auth` module entirely with `vi.mock('firebase/auth')` — mock `onAuthStateChanged` to capture the callback, then manually invoke it in tests to control auth state
       - Mock `firebase/firestore` for the ensureUserDocument calls (doc, setDoc, getDoc, etc.)
       - Mock `@/firebase` to provide a fake `auth` and `db` object
       - Use `setActivePinia(createPinia())` in beforeEach
       - Test each behavior listed above
       - For the auto-create test: mock `signInWithEmailAndPassword` to throw `{ code: 'auth/user-not-found' }`, verify `createUserWithEmailAndPassword` is then called

    2. Create `src/router/__tests__/router.test.ts`:
       - Mock the `getCurrentUser` function (it's defined in router/index.ts — either export it for testing or mock the onAuthStateChanged it wraps)
       - Use `router.push()` and check `router.currentRoute.value.path` after navigation
       - Test all four guard behaviors listed above

    3. Run tests — confirm they FAIL (RED).

    **Implement (GREEN):**

    4. Create `src/stores/auth.ts` — Pinia store using setup syntax (`defineStore('auth', () => {...})`):
       - `user` ref (User | null), `isReady` ref (boolean, starts false)
       - `onAuthStateChanged` listener: sets `user.value`, sets `isReady.value = true`
       - `isAuthenticated` computed
       - `loginWithGoogle()`: `signInWithPopup(auth, new GoogleAuthProvider())`. Catch `auth/popup-closed-by-user` and return null. On success, call `ensureUserDocument()` and return user.
       - `loginWithEmail(email, password)`: try `signInWithEmailAndPassword`. On `auth/user-not-found`, call `createUserWithEmailAndPassword` instead (locked decision: auto-create on first sign-in). On success, call `ensureUserDocument()` and return user.
       - `registerWithEmail(email, password)`: `createUserWithEmailAndPassword`. On success, call `ensureUserDocument()`.
       - `resetPassword(email)`: `sendPasswordResetEmail(auth, email)`
       - `logout()`: call cleanup on any active listeners, then `signOut(auth)`
       - `ensureUserDocument()`: After any successful login, write to `users/{uid}` with `{ email, displayName, photoURL, updatedAt }` using `setDoc` with `{ merge: true }`. Then check if `orgIds` array is empty or doc doesn't exist — if so, auto-create an organization: create `/organizations/{newOrgId}` with `{ name: displayName + "'s Church", createdAt, createdBy: uid }`, create `/organizations/{newOrgId}/members/{uid}` with `{ role: 'admin', joinedAt }`, and update user doc with `orgIds: [newOrgId]`. Use a Firestore batch write for atomicity.

    5. Create `src/router/index.ts`:
       - Define `getCurrentUser()` function: returns a Promise that resolves once `onAuthStateChanged` fires (listen once, then unsubscribe)
       - Routes: `/login` (lazy-load LoginView), `/` (lazy-load DashboardView, meta: `{ requiresAuth: true }`)
       - `beforeEach` guard: if `to.meta.requiresAuth` and no current user, redirect to `/login`. If going to `/login` and user IS authenticated, redirect to `/`.
       - Export router as default, also export `getCurrentUser` for testing

    6. Run tests — confirm they PASS (GREEN).
  </action>
  <verify>
    <automated>cd C:/projects/worshipplanner && npx vitest run src/stores/__tests__/auth.test.ts src/router/__tests__/router.test.ts --reporter=verbose 2>&1 | tail -30</automated>
  </verify>
  <done>
    - All auth store tests pass: loginWithGoogle, loginWithEmail (with auto-create), registerWithEmail, resetPassword, logout, isReady, isAuthenticated, ensureUserDocument
    - All router guard tests pass: unauthenticated redirect, authenticated pass-through, login-when-authenticated redirect, public route access
    - Auth store exports useAuthStore with all listed methods
    - Router exports default router + getCurrentUser
    - ensureUserDocument auto-creates org for new users
  </done>
</task>

<task type="auto" tdd="true">
  <name>Task 3: Firestore security rules tests against emulator</name>
  <files>
    src/rules.test.ts
  </files>
  <behavior>
    Security rules tests (using @firebase/rules-unit-testing against Firestore emulator):
    - Unauthenticated denial: unauthenticated client cannot read /organizations/{orgId} — request is denied
    - Unauthenticated denial: unauthenticated client cannot read /users/{uid} — request is denied
    - Org member read: authenticated user WITH a membership doc in /organizations/{orgId}/members/{uid} CAN read /organizations/{orgId}
    - Org member read: authenticated org member CAN read /organizations/{orgId}/members/{anyUid}
    - Org member read: authenticated org member CAN read /organizations/{orgId}/songs/{songId}
    - Cross-org denial: authenticated user who is a member of orgA CANNOT read /organizations/{orgB} (different org)
    - Cross-org denial: authenticated user who is a member of orgA CANNOT read /organizations/{orgB}/songs/{songId}
    - User profile isolation: authenticated user CAN read/write /users/{ownUid} but CANNOT read /users/{otherUid}
    - Admin write: org admin CAN write to /organizations/{orgId} and /organizations/{orgId}/members/{uid}
    - Non-admin write denial: org member with role "planner" (not admin) CANNOT write to /organizations/{orgId}
    - Catch-all denial: any request to an undefined path (e.g., /randomCollection/doc) is denied
  </behavior>
  <action>
    **Write tests first (RED):**

    1. Create `src/rules.test.ts` using `@firebase/rules-unit-testing` v2 API:
       - Import `initializeTestEnvironment`, `assertSucceeds`, `assertFails`, `RulesTestEnvironment` from `@firebase/rules-unit-testing`
       - Import `readFileSync` from `fs` to load `firestore.rules` from disk
       - In `beforeAll`: call `initializeTestEnvironment({ projectId: 'test-project', firestore: { rules: readFileSync('firestore.rules', 'utf8'), host: '127.0.0.1', port: 8080 } })` — store the returned `testEnv`
       - In `afterEach`: call `testEnv.clearFirestore()` to reset data between tests
       - In `afterAll`: call `testEnv.cleanup()`

       **Test setup helpers:**
       - `getAuthenticatedContext(uid)`: `testEnv.authenticatedContext(uid)` — returns a context with auth.uid set
       - `getUnauthenticatedContext()`: `testEnv.unauthenticatedContext()` — returns unauthenticated context
       - `seedMembershipDoc(orgId, uid, role)`: use `testEnv.withSecurityRulesDisabled(async (context) => { ... })` to write a membership doc `/organizations/{orgId}/members/{uid}` with `{ role, joinedAt }` — this bypasses rules to set up test fixtures

       **Test cases — implement each as a separate `it()` block:**

       describe("unauthenticated access"):
       - "denies unauthenticated read on /organizations/{orgId}": get unauthenticated context, assertFails on getDoc of any org doc
       - "denies unauthenticated read on /users/{uid}": assertFails on getDoc of any user doc

       describe("org member access"):
       - "allows org member to read their org": seed membership doc for (orgA, userA, 'admin'), get authenticated context for userA, assertSucceeds on getDoc of /organizations/orgA
       - "allows org member to read members subcollection": seed membership, assertSucceeds on getDoc of /organizations/orgA/members/userA
       - "allows org member to read nested collections (songs)": seed membership, seed a song doc (via withSecurityRulesDisabled), assertSucceeds on getDoc of /organizations/orgA/songs/song1

       describe("cross-org isolation"):
       - "denies cross-org read": seed membership for (orgA, userA), assertFails when userA tries to getDoc /organizations/orgB
       - "denies cross-org nested collection read": seed membership for (orgA, userA), assertFails when userA tries to getDoc /organizations/orgB/songs/song1

       describe("user profile isolation"):
       - "allows user to read own profile": assertSucceeds when userA reads /users/userA
       - "denies user from reading another user profile": assertFails when userA reads /users/userB

       describe("admin vs member write permissions"):
       - "allows admin to write org doc": seed membership (orgA, userA, 'admin'), assertSucceeds on setDoc /organizations/orgA
       - "denies planner from writing org doc": seed membership (orgA, userA, 'planner'), assertFails on setDoc /organizations/orgA

       describe("catch-all deny"):
       - "denies access to undefined paths": assertFails when authenticated user reads /randomCollection/randomDoc

    2. Run tests against emulator — confirm they FAIL (RED) since rules are already written in Task 1, tests should actually PASS. If using TDD strictly: write tests, verify they connect to emulator and execute, then confirm GREEN.

    **IMPORTANT implementation notes:**
    - This test file runs against a LIVE Firestore emulator, not mocks. The emulator must be running.
    - The verify command uses `firebase emulators:exec` which starts the emulator, runs the test, and shuts down — no need for a separately running emulator.
    - Use `fs.readFileSync` with a relative path `'firestore.rules'` (resolved from project root where vitest runs).
    - Do NOT import from `@/firebase` — this test uses `@firebase/rules-unit-testing` directly, not the app's Firebase initialization.
  </action>
  <verify>
    <automated>cd C:/projects/worshipplanner && firebase emulators:exec "npx vitest run src/rules.test.ts --reporter=verbose" --only firestore 2>&1 | tail -30</automated>
  </verify>
  <done>
    - src/rules.test.ts exists with 11+ test cases covering all security rules truths
    - All tests pass when run via `firebase emulators:exec`
    - Unauthenticated access is denied (organizations and users)
    - Org member can read own org data (org doc, members, songs)
    - Cross-org access is denied (both org doc and nested collections)
    - User profile isolation works (own profile only)
    - Admin can write org doc; non-admin planner cannot
    - Catch-all denies undefined paths
  </done>
</task>

</tasks>

<verification>
- `npm run build` completes with zero errors
- `npx vitest run src/stores/__tests__/auth.test.ts src/router/__tests__/router.test.ts` — all unit tests pass
- `firebase emulators:exec "npx vitest run src/rules.test.ts" --only firestore` — all security rules tests pass
- `npx vue-tsc --noEmit` — no TypeScript errors
- `firestore.rules` file exists with deny-by-default rules and org membership checks

<success_criteria>
- Project scaffolded with Vue 3, TypeScript, Pinia, Vue Router, Vitest, Tailwind v4, Firebase
- Firebase singleton module exports app, auth, db with emulator support
- Pinia auth store handles Google sign-in, email sign-in (with auto-create), password reset, logout
- Auth store auto-creates Firestore user document and default org on first sign-in
- Router guard prevents unauthenticated access to protected routes
- Router guard redirects authenticated users away from /login
- Firestore security rules enforce deny-by-default with org membership checks
- All unit tests pass (auth store + router guard)
- All security rules tests pass against emulator (unauthenticated denial, org member access, cross-org denial, user isolation, admin write permissions, catch-all deny)
</success_criteria>
